description_long: This is a roughly triangular stone block with a rugged, irregular surface and a weathered appearance. One face features a series of deep, diagonal grooves or indentations, suggesting it may have been shaped or worn by repeated contact or use. The other sides are more natural and uneven, with pitted textures and some smoother, flatter areas. The coloration is predominantly gray with subtle variations, typical of concrete or weathered stone. The block appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a street or park.
description: A rugged, triangular gray stone block with deep grooves on one face and rough, weathered surfaces all around. It appears dense, solid, and suitable as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: This view highlights the grooved face with deep, diagonal indentations and a rough, angular surface.
description_view_1: This side shows a more vertical, elongated profile with irregular, pitted textures and no visible grooves.
description_view_2: The back face is relatively flat and smoother compared to the other sides, with subtle surface texture.
description_view_3: This edge view reveals the block's thickness and uneven, jagged contours, lacking any prominent grooves.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: The block shows signs of surface erosion and pitting, with some edges appearing chipped and worn, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
